name: Ponte Caldelas and Cotobade Loop
name_original: Ponte Caldelas - vuelta por Borela y Cotobade
description: This moderate 20.8-mile road cycling route near Ponte Caldelas offers scenic views, historical landmarks, and tranquil eucalyptus plantation
region_intro: Road cycling around Ponte Caldelas offers diverse terrain in the province of Pontevedra, Galicia. The region is characterized by the meandering River Verdugo, which provides riverside paths, and varying elevations that lead to viewpoints such as Mirador de Castrelada. Cyclists can explore routes through dense forests, including a unique sequoia forest, and along the Serra da Castrelada mountains. This blend of river valleys and elevated areas creates a varied landscape suitable for different road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Ponte Caldelas The most popular road cycling route is Ponte Caldelas and Cotobade Loop, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views and varied terrain, including eucalyptus plantations. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Parque Monte de Seixo and Sierra de Cando Vuelta, a difficult 39.9 miles (64.3 km) path. This route features challenging climbs and panoramic views over the surrounding mountains and reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Borela Bridge loop from Pontevedra, a 16.1 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through picturesque riverside paths, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Road cycling around Ponte Caldelas is defined by the scenic River Verdugo, varied elevations leading to viewpoints, and routes through dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Ponte Caldelas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ponte Caldelas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Ponte Caldelas offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with nearly 200 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 6 easy, 91 moderate, and 97 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The region features diverse terrain, from riverside paths along the &lt;strong&gt;River Verdugo&lt;/strong&gt; to challenging climbs leading to panoramic viewpoints like the &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de Castrelada&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Alto de Xuntáns&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll encounter dense forests, including a unique sequoia forest, and routes through the Serra da Castrelada mountains, offering varied landscapes for every ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Ponte Caldelas?
A: Yes, Ponte Caldelas offers several circular road cycling routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975122304/ponte-caldelas-and-cotobade-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Caldelas and Cotobade Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.5 km long with 480m of elevation gain. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31793902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borela Bridge loop from Pontevedra&lt;/a&gt;, covering 26.0 km.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region has many difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963295054/parque-monte-de-seixo-and-sierra-de-cando-vuelta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Monte de Seixo and Sierra de Cando Vuelta&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 64.3 km route with over 1150m of elevation gain, offering panoramic views. Another difficult option is the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach – Borela Bridge loop from Buchabade&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 120 km with more than 2100m of ascent.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Ponte Caldelas is rich in cultural and historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its historical significance, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4490085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient stepping stones at "Os Pasos" in A Fraga, historic water mills, and unique "Casas de Indianos" architecture.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes lead to stunning viewpoints. The &lt;strong&gt;Mirador de Castrelada&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Alto de Xuntáns&lt;/strong&gt; (over 400 meters high) offer panoramic views of the River Verdugo, the Serra da Castrelada mountains, the Eiras reservoir, and even the Vigo estuary. These elevated areas provide visually rewarding cycling experiences.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The natural landscape is dominated by the &lt;strong&gt;River Verdugo&lt;/strong&gt;, with its tree-lined banks and spots like the &lt;strong&gt;A Calzada fluvial beach&lt;/strong&gt;. You can also cycle near the unique &lt;strong&gt;sequoia forest in Monte do Pé da Múa&lt;/strong&gt;, where some trees exceed 40 meters in height, or explore paths that merge with trails like "As Almiñas and Foxo do Lobo".

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Ponte Caldelas?
A: The road cycling routes in Ponte Caldelas are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, scenic views, and the variety of options available for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any riverside cycling paths in the area?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;River Verdugo&lt;/strong&gt; meanders through the region, offering beautiful riverside paths. The &lt;strong&gt;Sendeiro Azul (Blue Trail) of the River Verdugo&lt;/strong&gt; borders the river and connects to picturesque spots like "Os Pasos da Fraga," providing scenic routes that can be enjoyed by bike, often merging with other local paths.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Ponte Caldelas allow you to experience both. For instance, you can cycle through areas near the &lt;strong&gt;River Verdugo&lt;/strong&gt; and then visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/450468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Sampaio Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4490085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;. The region's blend of natural landscapes and historical points of interest makes for a rich cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for a longer, multi-hour ride?
A: For longer rides, consider routes that explore the broader region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1271254" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach – Borela Bridge loop from O Piñeiro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.1 km route, typically taking over 2.5 hours. For an even more extensive challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach – Borela Bridge loop from Buchabade&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 120.1 km route, ideal for a full day of cycling.
tips: River beach on the Verdugo river, with safe and quiet parking close to restaurants, cafes and bars

It's not every day you cycle past an old aqueduct. It is located on the outskirts of the town of Mirón, in the municipality of Ponte Caldeas, on the border with the municipality of Cotobade. The origin is unknown, but it was used to redirect water for the fields and the animals of the town.

Borela Bridge, next to the hermitage of Lourdes, in Cotobade. Built over the Almofrei river, this bridge dates back to the 17th century, although we would go back to the 15th century since it was originally a medieval bridge
Very quiet and safe 6 km downhill section surrounded by eucalyptus plantations towards Ponte Caldelas

faqs.question: Where does the road cycling route start?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts near the Ponte Caldelas River Beach, which is a pleasant spot on the Verdugo River. This area serves as a convenient starting point for your ride.

faqs.question: How challenging is this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as moderate. While it features mostly well-paved surfaces, the 33.5-kilometer distance and approximately 480 meters of elevation gain suggest a mix of climbs and descents. It requires a good level of fitness, but there's a notable 6 km downhill section through eucalyptus plantations that offers a relaxing stretch.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Ponte Caldelas - return via Borela and Cotobade" route is designed as a loop, bringing you back to your starting point in Ponte Caldelas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a beautiful blend of natural landscapes and historical points. Expect to cycle through tranquil eucalyptus plantations, especially on the downhill sections, and enjoy views of the Galician countryside. The route also passes by the Ponte Caldelas River Beach at the start/end.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Key landmarks include the historic Mirón Aqueduct, located near the edge of Mirón, and the 17th-century Borela Bridge, which crosses the Almofrei River near the hermitage of Lourdes in Cotobade.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the best weather conditions and scenery, the spring and autumn months are generally ideal for cycling in Galicia. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring more rain.

faqs.question: Is the Ponte Caldelas - return via Borela and Cotobade route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, distance of 33.5 km, and elevation gain, this route is best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness. It might be too challenging for young children or families looking for a very leisurely ride.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it's generally not designed for accompanying pets. If you bring a dog, ensure it's safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always follow local leash laws and clean-up regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle in Galicia, Spain?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or entrance fees to cycle this route in Galicia, Spain, as it primarily uses public roads and trails.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Ponte Caldelas, being the starting and ending point, offers various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. You may also find smaller establishments in Borela and Cotobade, though options might be more limited.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails. Notably, it covers about 19% of both the Mtb Vía Mariana and the Via Mariana, and smaller portions of the Ruta das Almiñas e foxo do lobo, Caldas de Reis - Ruta, Sendeiro Rural de Galicia, and Ponte Borela.
